title: UC Santa Cruz Library publishes vast photo archive from iconic ‘Death of a Valley’
summary: UC Santa Cruz Library has digitized and made publicly available 3,200 images taken by Dorothea Lange and Pirkle Jones from their photo project that captured the final year of Monticello.

summary: Bookshop Santa Cruz will host the celebrated novelist and Emeritus Professor of Literature Karen Tei Yamashita for the launch of her new book, Questions 27 & 28, in conversation with History Professor Alice Yang.

title: Open comments: UC Police Dept. policies
summary: The University of California Office of the President invites comments on the proposed revisions to thirty-three University of California Police Department (UCPD) policies

title: In Memoriam: Maxine Lane (1926-2026)
summary: UC Santa Cruz retiree Maxine Lane died on April 17, 2026 at 99 years old. Maxine worked at the campus for 22 years and was a staunch supporter and advocate for UC Santa Cruz veteran students.

title: Anna Tsing and Terrie Williams named to American Academy of Arts & Sciences
summary: The two faculty members are among the 252 leaders in academia, the arts, industry, journalism, philanthropy, policy, research, and science elected this year

title: Spring 2026 Campus Elections: ballot information and voting details
summary: Student participation in campus elections is vital to ensuring a vibrant, representative, and inclusive campus community. By voting, students select future leaders and help shape key decisions affecting student life at UC Santa Cruz.

title: Senate Faculty Support Survey now open: Share your experiences
summary: The survey is designed to help Academic Affairs and the Office for Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (ODEI) understand how to improve their programs that support faculty research, teaching, and service.

title: Thousands of future Slugs gather at UC Santa Cruz for Banana Slug Day
summary: With tours, lectures, a campus welcome, and a lively resource fair, Banana Slug Day gave admitted students an immersive look at academics, support services, and student life, helping them picture their future at UC Santa Cruz.

title: Story maps show massive fires like CZU are not a new phenomenon for Santa Cruz
summary: An anthropology project traces the history of land use and fire in the county, revealing lessons for future management and mitigation

summary: The UCSC OneCard is the university’s preferred payment method for eligible expenses, and it’s designed to save you time, reduce paperwork, and eliminate out-of-pocket costs.

title: New method to raise investment funds for projects that restore coastal wetlands for climate adaptation
summary: Center for Coastal Climate Resilience partners with The Nature Conservancy and others on a first-of-its-kind tool to drive private and public investment in adaptation built by nature

title: The language of food: entrepreneur Kendra Baker (Crown, ‘01, language studies) is this year’s Humanities Alumni Award honoree
summary: Kendra Baker, co-founder of the Penny Creamery and The Picnic Basket, is this year’s UC Santa Cruz Distinguished Humanities Alumni Award recipient, an honor that highlights a career shaped by the study of language and culture.

title: Threat of California’s native tree loss is greater than current estimates
summary: New study finds that many of the state’s valuable and most recognizable trees could decline sooner than expected because current risk calculations don’t incorporate climate change

title: Prioritizing your well-being this spring quarter
summary: Spring quarter can feel demanding. Prioritize your mental health, stay connected, and access support resources available to help you navigate this stretch.

title: Mother’s diet and environment may disrupt children’s metabolism

title: Alumna Gillian Welch brings Dead-driven Acoustic Reckoning show to Quarry Amphitheater
summary: A packed Quarry Amphitheater was filled with fresh interpretations of songs from across the Grateful Dead’s vast catalogue with audience members singing, dancing and cheering along

title: Researchers to measure atmospheric benefits of restored San Francisco Bay wetland
summary: UC Santa Cruz will lend expertise in monitoring tidal marsh carbon levels once a 275-acre South Bay salt pond is converted back to its natural state, as part of a larger environmental campaign by multiple partners to restore lost tidal wetlands to San Francisco Bay.

title: STEM Culture Festival 5/15/26
summary: Join us for the STEM Culture Festival, which will take place in the Baskin Engineering Courtyard on Friday, May 15, 2026 at 1pm. All are welcome! This event will feature: Musical performances & dance lessons, STEM-themed drag shows, inspirational talks, student organization tabling, raffles for prizes, and FREE delicious food for all attendees.

title: NSF awards 20 students and alumni with highly coveted Graduate Research Fellowships
summary: The fellowship program is one of the nation’s most prestigious of its kind. Awardees at or from UC Santa Cruz represent fields ranging from engineering to the physical, biological, and social sciences.

title: Baskin Engineering Climate Week is here! Explore the events.
summary: Baskin Engineering Climate Week is focused on raising awareness of climate issues and sustainability research and teaching. We’ll have student organization demos and opportunities to explore cutting-edge research and pathways into careers in climate technology and sustainability—all while creating space to connect, collaborate, and learn from one another. Come join the events!

title: Candidate interviews for Vice Provost and Dean of Undergraduate Education
summary: All candidates will present a town hall talk that will also be recorded for later viewing. At the scheduled times, candidates will give a 30–40-minute talk followed by real-time moderated Q&A.

title: Alumna and Anthropic co-founder Daniela Amodei and civil-rights activist Dolores Huerta named in Time’s 100 Most Influential People of 2026
summary: Daniela Amodei graduated from UC Santa Cruz in 2009 with a B.A. in literature and a minor in politics. In 2022, UCSC’s Research Center for the Americas was renamed in honor of social justice icon Dolores Huerta; the university continues to collaborate with the Dolores Huerta Foundation.

title: New UCPath feature: Emergency contact for immigration enforcement workplace detention or arrest
summary: UCPath has introduced a new emergency contact category specifically for immigration enforcement detention or arrests that occur at work.

title: Acclaimed astrophysicist and electric slam poet blend science and storytelling
summary: Slam poet Jasmine Schlafke and UC Santa Cruz professor Enrico Ramirez-Ruiz blend science, poetry and storytelling to explore our deepest origins and shared humanity at the second annual Landesman Lecture on April 28.

title: UC Santa Cruz debuts on CodeSignal’s University Report
summary: A new ranking demonstrates Banana Slugs’ software engineering career readiness

title: DESI completes largest high-resolution 3D map of universe to date
summary: The Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) has completed its originally planned five-year mission and mapped more than 47 million galaxies and quasars, and will continue observations into 2028 and further expand the map.

title: United by loss, a Palestinian and Israeli walk together on a path to peace
summary: Aziz Abu Sarah, a Palestinian peacebuilder, and Maoz Inon, an Israeli entrepreneur and activist, will speak on Monday, April 20, in Santa Cruz about their work promoting understanding and reconciliation in the region.

title: A Deep Read interview with Merlin Sheldrake, author of Entangled Life
summary: The British biologist Merlin Sheldrake, this year’s featured author for The Humanities Institute’s (THI) seventh annual Deep Read, spoke with UC Santa Cruz about the strange, generative intelligence of fungi and what it can teach us about connection, creativity, and survival.

title: April in Santa Cruz: Experimental music festival explores new traditions
summary: The annual April in Santa Cruz Festival of Creative Music (AiSC) returns this year with UC Santa Cruz faculty and graduate students, renowned guest artists, and several visiting artists.

title: Rally for California Science (SB 895) on May 4
summary: If passed, SB 895 would place a $23 billion bond to fund scientific research on California’s November 2026 ballot.

title: In memoriam: Dennis Wake
summary: Dennis was a well-loved member of the UCSC Dining team and dedicated over 22 years to feeding the campus community.

title: National award recognizes UCSC’s student-centered housing strategy
summary: Where students live shapes their success. UC Santa Cruz built a housing plan around that premise—and it earned a national award.

summary: For interested students, we compete in competitions that work with large-scale computing clusters, as well as being a club for students who want to work with distributed computation. title: Art, ecology, and interconnection: UC Santa Cruz launches inaugural Earth Day festival
summary: Professor Jennifer Parker organized the April 22 Arts & Ecology Festival as a space for gathering, exchange, and collective inquiry, bringing together interdisciplinary research, visual and sonic practices, and performance to engage the urgencies of the environmental crisis through shared experience and collaboration

title: With expertise spanning media, health, AI, and energy, new leaders join UC Santa Cruz Foundation Board of Trustees
summary: The UC Santa Cruz Foundation Board of Trustees works in partnership with university leadership to advance philanthropic support for the campus mission while fostering greater understanding of UC Santa Cruz’s research, education, and public impact.

title: Earth Month: Celebrating our campus, our progress, and a shared sustainable future
summary: As we kick off Earth Month, I’m pleased to share some highlights of our community’s progress on sustainability and environmental stewardship.

title: UCAD Plus Joint Task Force Update #3
summary: Each of these groups has diligently identified creative ways to leverage existing strengths and external partnerships to improve efficiency and preserve and expand capacity amid ongoing disruptions.

title: Incoming: New fellow will probe moon’s magnetic past for clues about its formation and interior
summary: 51 Pegasi b Fellow Sarah Steele will spend three years at UC Santa Cruz working with Earth and planetary scientists to advance her work to decode ancient magnetic records preserved in rocks

title: UC Santa Cruz pledges to become a “Clean California Community,” expands commitment to statewide beautification and environmental stewardship
summary: The Clean California initiative is an effort to remove litter, restore public spaces, and inspire community engagement across California.

title: New reference cards for responding to immigration officials in “limited access” spaces
summary: These cards describe steps to take in the event that immigration officers request access to a limited-access space such as a classroom when class is in session or a laboratory.

summary: All candidates will present a town hall talk that will also be recorded for later viewing. At the scheduled times, candidates will give a 30–40-minute talk followed by real-time moderated Q&A.

title: UC Santa Cruz among top 10 public U.S. universities with high-impact research, analysis shows
summary: The campus ranks among the top 10 public universities in the United States home to high-impact researchers, according to a recent analysis by Clarivate.

title: Notice of Open Comment: PACAOS 100 & Appendix H, Investigation and Resolution
summary: The UC Office of the President invites comments on proposed revisions to the Presidential Policy Applying to Campus Activities, Organizations and Students

title: Jennifer Johnson-Hanks to serve as Campus Provost and Executive Vice Chancellor
summary: Through a range of roles over 25 years at UC Berkeley, Johnson-Hanks has established a record of collaborative and transformative leadership in the service of excellent and inclusive public education.

title: Engineering student wins NVIDIA Agents for Impact Hackathon
summary: Preet Karia built an AI-powered tool to turn physics notes into 3D visualizations.

title: Spring quarter student newsletter: Go Bananas! with The Peel
summary: The Division of Student Affairs and Success welcomes students to spring quarter with the first installment of The Peel, a new newsletter for 2025-26.

title: UCSC alumna Quressa Robinson expands representation in publishing
summary: As a literary agent, Quressa Robinson (Oakes ‘03, literature) uses her influence to bring diverse, nuanced stories to young adult readers.

title: Dr. Ramon Resa: Building a life of service in the Central Valley
summary: Ramon Resa (Crown ’76, biology) grew up picking crops in California’s Central Valley and went on to spend over four decades as a pediatrician in the community he calls home. A UC Santa Cruz alumnus, Distinguished Alumni Award recipient, and author, Resa has built a life defined by service.

title: Long days, snacks, and scan-a-thons: student archivists stepped up to save a treasure trove of Okinawan-American memories
summary: In a cramped conference room, a small group of students spent countless hours digitizing the photos, slides and artworks of the Okinawan American community in Los Angeles County. These students work for the Okinawa Memories Initiative.

title: Making ethics accessible
summary: Alumnus Tim Shannon (Cowell ’71, history) has philanthropically supported the Humanities at UC Santa Cruz for 15 years. His support of the Norcal Regional Highschool Ethics Bowl, hosted annually at UC Santa Cruz, has opened doors for public schools across the region.

title: Grant funds research to identify drug targets for autism and schizophrenia using human stem cell models
summary: The research aims to  uncover the molecular and cellular mechanisms underlying autism and schizophrenia and to identify drug targets for both conditions.

title: Three UC Santa Cruz professor-student teams win Keck Foundation funding
summary: The program provides funds to support the career trajectories of early- to mid-career faculty and their graduate students.

title: New oral history chronicles life and legacy of sociologist John Brown Childs
summary: The oral history traces a life shaped by what Childs has called the “two mighty currents” of his heritage.

title: ASE-GSR contract agreement
summary: Agreements that cover our Academic Student Employees (ASEs) and Graduate Student Researchers (GSRs) are now in effect and extend through December 31, 2029.

title: CHI 2026 UC Santa Cruz Contribution
summary: The Department of Computational Media at the Baskin School of Engineering at UC Santa Cruz will contribute a wide range of publications to CHI 2026, the flagship annual conference for human-computer interaction and design.

title: Finding direction through creativity: The Arts Division’s Find Your Path! creates career pathways for students
summary: Arts Division students are invited to presentations and networking sessions that will guide them on how to pursue a successful career.

title: Two UC Santa Cruz faculty members named 2025 AAAS fellows
summary: Katharyne Mitchell and Adina Paytan were awarded the lifetime honor.

title: SAB Staff Appreciation Picnic – Save the date and tabling information
summary: Please save the date- Monday, May 11 – for the annual SAB Staff Appreciation Picnic and Raffle. We are excited to welcome you all to this event to celebrate everyone’s hard work this year.

title: UC Santa Cruz trio takes third at 2026 Mistral AI hackathon with cybersecurity platform
summary: A team of UC Santa Cruz students and an alumnus earned third place at the 2026 Mistral AI Worldwide Hackathon in San Francisco for developing an artificial intelligence-powered cybersecurity solution to help companies prevent phishing attacks.

title: Damming the Nile and the devastating cost of human progress
summary: In her upcoming Nauenberg History of Science Lecture, World Wounds: The Damming of the Nile River and the Transformation of Medicine, Associate Professor of History Jennifer Derr will explore how massive infrastructure projects contributed to widespread illness, and offer insights into the global history of medicine.

title: Baskin Engineering Dean Alexander Wolf to step down
summary: Over the past decade, Dean Wolf spearheaded a major transformation of Baskin Engineering, strengthening the school’s academic foundation, expanding its research impact, elevating its international profile, and setting it on a course for even greater success in the years to come.

title: College academic leadership update
summary: College academic leadership updates include new provost appointments and a pilot model pairing colleges under five full-time provosts starting July 1, 2026.

title: UC Santa Cruz awarded grant to promote and support open scholarship, engaged mentoring
summary: UC Santa Cruz will launch a pioneering initiative to support and promote open science, open scholarship, and high-quality graduate student mentoring by developing guidelines to evaluate and recognize this important work.

title: UC Santa Cruz’s deep expertise with elephant seals vital to avian influenza monitoring
summary: Well-trained students and scientists are the boots on the beach collecting the samples and observations needed to help a statewide collaboration of responders monitor the recent outbreak

title: With fastest solver and fourth place for D1 schools, UC Santa Cruz finishes strong at 2025 Codebreaker Challenge
summary: The challenge tasks students to develop their reverse engineering and cryptography skills using a realistic cybersecurity scenario

title: Exploring the impacts of California’s minimum wage for fast food workers
summary: Workers at franchised fast food restaurants got a big pay bump about two years ago. A UC Santa Cruz economics researcher suggests that may have come with a side of unintended consequences.

title: An adventurous vulture’s plight
summary: Disturbing increases in toxic lead exposure are linked to wider foraging by the critically endangered California condor and more wild pigs being shot throughout the state, masking positive effects of outreach and lead-ammo bans

title: Tentative Agreement on ASE/GSR Contract
summary: Below is a letter sent on behalf of UC System Provost and Executive Vice President Katherine S. Newman regarding the tentative agreement the University of California reached with the United Auto Workers (UAW)

title: Five essential bicycle safety tips for Slugs
summary: With warm and sunny weather, the UC Santa Cruz community is more active, with many community members walking and biking across campus for classes, meetings, and events.

title: Reminder regarding international remote work for academic appointees
summary: This communication serves as a reminder of the University of California’s prohibition on remote work from outside of the United States.

title: Expert in assistive technology Professor Sri Kurniawan awarded 2025-26 Sung-Mo Steve Kang DEI Award
summary: The Baskin School of Engineering recognized Sri Kurniawan, professor of computational media and associate dean for research at Baskin Engineering as the 2025-26 recipient of the Sung-Mo Steve Kang DEI Award. This annual award recognizes a faculty member who is making meaningful strides toward more inclusive teaching and education at Baskin Engineering.

title: Following rigorous review, WASC reaffirms UC Santa Cruz accreditation
summary: Being accredited means UC Santa Cruz has met rigorous standards for quality, capacity, and effectiveness.

title: $2 million grant to propel UC Santa Cruz humanities careers
summary: Since launching in 2023, the Employing Humanities initiative has provided hundreds of undergraduates with real-world work experience—as resource development coordinators, editorial writers, digital marketing interns, and archivists—that helps prepare them for impactful careers after they graduate.Underscoring the initiative’s impact, UCSC was just awarded a $2 million grant from the Mellon Foundation.

title: UCSC’s Right Livelihood Center empowers the next generation of activists
summary: The Right Livelihood Center at UC Santa Cruz offers students a range of opportunities to connect with global visionaries. Students Natalie Capobres (Kresge ’26, environmental studies) and Kevin Lin (Kresge ’26, community studies) share their experiences.

title: UC Santa Cruz advances partnerships in Indonesia, formalizes first 2+2 agreement
summary: UC Santa Cruz faculty and staff traveled to Indonesia in February to sign the campus’s first 2+2 degree agreement with the University of Indonesia and explore additional academic partnerships with universities in Jakarta and Bandung.

title: Parking will be limited in Hahn Lot (Lot 101) from April 1 – 10
summary: April 1–10, Hahn Lot (Lot 101) will have limited parking during business hours as UC Santa Cruz welcomes more than 5,000 admitted students and families to campus.

title: Father’s tobacco use may raise children’s diabetes risk
summary: Mouse study finds link between father’s nicotine exposure and offspring’s metabolism

title: ‘Dignidad en los campos’: un reporte documenta las condiciones y presenta recomendaciones para los trabajadores agrícolas de Salinas.
summary: El reporte ofrece soluciones prácticas y centradas en la equidad para las dificultades que limitan tanto el bienestar de los trabajadores como la prosperidad regional a largo plazo.

title: ‘Dignity in the Fields’: report documents conditions and outlines recommendations for Salinas farmworkers
summary: The report provides practical, equity-centered solutions for the challenges that limit both worker well-being and long-term regional prosperity.

title: Humanities EXPLORE Fellows bridge history, culture, and community
summary: This year’s Humanities EXPLORE Program fellows are adventurous and ambitious, transcribing 300-year–old texts, helping to revitalize forgotten languages, exploring archives, and learning from faculty mentors. This winter, two fellows were given film cameras to preserve and share their experiences of connection, creativity, and purpose.

title: Does sexual harassment behavior matter for ecosystems?
summary: Intense harassment of females by male mosquitofish increases the ecosystem consequences of this highly invasive fish

title: Maria Rocha-Ruiz named UC Santa Cruz’s Sea Dubs Wavemaker
summary: Maria Rocha-Ruiz has been named UC Santa Cruz’s Sea Dubs Wavemaker, honoring her leadership expanding educational opportunity and mentoring generations of students across the Central Coast.

title: Invest in student success: Donate to the Career Clothing Closet
summary: UCSC Career Success helps students prepare for interviews and internships through its Clothing Closet, offering free professional attire each quarter and welcoming donations to support student success.

title: Against all odds, popular math animator Grant Sanderson wooed into the redwoods
summary: Mathematics Department convinced famous YouTuber to speak on campus, enthralling a full house with visualizations of high-dimensional geometry that powers large language models

title: Engineered imagination
summary: UC Santa Cruz alumna Lisa Winter (Porter ’09, art) brings an artist’s eye to the arena of combat robotics—evolving from childhood Robot Wars competitor to BattleBots judge and robotics entrepreneur.

title: Two Baskin Engineering graduate students broaden access to research and inclusive AI with the Fellowship for Anti-Racism Research (FARR)
summary: Two UC Santa Cruz students are working to make STEM more equitable with support from the Baskin Engineering Fellowship for Anti-Racism Research (FARR). Since 2021, FARR has awarded $6,000 in funding to two graduate students whose research aims to dismantle racism and bias in technology and engineering.

title: Campus releases second quarter financial summary, with updates on state, federal budgets
summary: I write to share details about our second quarter financial summary, the state budget proposed by Gov. Gavin Newsom, and the federal funding package approved in February.

title: In Memoriam: David Couzens Hoy
summary: Hoy had a world-renowned reputation as a scholar in Continental philosophy and critical theory.

title: In memoriam: Philanthropist and civic leader Patricia “Pat” Rebele
summary: It is with sadness that I share the news that Patricia “Pat” Rebele, a proud UC Santa Cruz alumna, dedicated philanthropist, and longtime advocate for the arts, education, and social justice, passed away Dec. 15 at the age of 96.

title: Philanthropist and arts advocate Pat Rebele dies at age 96
summary: Patricia “Pat” Rebele’s quiet generosity helped shape Santa Cruz County for decades.

title: Call for Staff Advisory Board Self-nominations
summary: Are you interested in serving our campus community and giving back to your fellow staff members? Consider joining the UCSC Staff Advisory Board (SAB)!

title: Supposedly harmless peptide may be linked to Alzheimer’s disease
summary: New paper from UC Santa Cruz chemistry lab argues that efforts to develop treatments for Alzheimer’s should stop ignoring a short protein called P3, the ‘neglected cousin’ of amyloid beta

title: In Crown College’s innovation lab, students work on industry-driven challenges
summary: In Crown College’s Corporate Innovation Laboratory (CRWN 102), students like Evan Rantala and Julien Howard address industry-driven challenges alongside tech and community partners. The course, part of Crown’s Innovation & Entrepreneurship Certificate, pairs academic learning with hands-on experience that extends beyond the classroom.

title: UCSC Dining Services takes first place at the Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk Clam Chowder Cook-Off
summary: UCSC Dining Services took 1st place in Boston Chowder and 3rd in Manhattan Chowder at the Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk Clam Chowder Cook-Off.

title: About the UC Santa Cruz elephant seal research program
summary: The UC Santa Cruz elephant seal research program began in 1967 when Professor Emeritus Burney Le Boeuf initiated a systematic tagging and censusing project at the Año Nuevo Reserve. This long-term study is housed within the Department of Ecology and Evolutionary Biology (EEB). Currently, the research is spearheaded by Assistant Professor Roxanne Beltran, with significant […]

title: Policies on Faculty Rights and Responsibilities webpage
summary: The page brings together relevant Regents policies, related Academic Personnel Manual provisions, and campus Academic Senate policies that guide faculty rights and responsibilities.

title: Digital accessibility: where to start before April
summary: This isn’t only about compliance. It’s about our shared responsibility to ensure people with disabilities have access to all of UC Santa Cruz’s services, programs, and activities.

title: First cases of highly pathogenic avian influenza in northern elephant seals confirmed in California
summary: The outbreak marks the first cases of HPAI H5N1 in marine mammals in California and the first detection in northern elephant seals.

title: Ancient DNA reveals Ice Age mammals thrived after volcanic eruption
summary: UC Santa Cruz researchers were able to collect ancient DNA preserved in permafrost for insight into how a natural disaster impacted genetic diversity.

title: Humanities skills are transferable skills
summary: UC Santa Cruz humanities students graduate with a toolkit of transferable skills in high demand across fields ranging from academia to the entertainment industry and high-tech startups. Here are the stories of UC Santa Cruz humanities graduates thriving in a wide range of jobs.

title: In Memoriam: Todd Newberry
summary: A beloved teacher with a conviction in the power of a liberal arts education, Newberry inspired thousands of students to approach life with both wonder and, most importantly, incisive questions.

title: From climate resilience to forensic science, 2025 Innovation Impact Award recipients announced
summary: Their work demonstrates how UC Santa Cruz innovation moves from discovery to real-world benefit.

title: Grants support DIY irrigation and smart, electric tractors for specialty crop growers
summary: Two projects are receiving support from the USDA 2025 Specialty Crop Block Grant Program.

title: Engineering education in the age of AI
summary: Artificial intelligence is reshaping the world our students are preparing to enter. It is transforming how we design products, diagnose disease, model climate systems, and move information across the globe. It is also transforming the workforce itself. The question before us is not whether AI will change engineering education. It already has. The question is how universities and industry can utilize AI to support engineers in the classroom and beyond.

title: UC Santa Cruz & HFFD: Breaking records and feeding hope, once again
summary: The community donated enough to provide healthy food to 846 people all year long!

title: Alumni-founded biotech firm enters partnership to advance treatment for undruggable targets
summary: Unnatural Products, a biotech company founded by UC Santa Cruz alumni Cameron Pye (Ph.D. ’17, chemistry & biochemistry) and Joshua Schwochert (Ph.D. ’17, chemistry & biochemistry), opens up treatment possibilities for diseases once thought to be undruggable.

title: In Memoriam – Teresa de Lauretis (1938-2026)
summary: A pioneeringthinker who brought semiotics and psychoanalytic theory to bear upon cinematic representationsof desire, de Lauretis taught at the University of California, Santa Cruz, from the mid-1980s untilher retirement in 2008 as Distinguished Professor Emerita.

title: Baskin Engineering students win in sustainability, justice, and education projects at CruzHacks 2026
summary: CruzHacks 2026, UC Santa Cruz’s premier annual hackathon, brought together more than 300 students across 90 teams to engineer solutions with positive social impact. Notably, Baskin School of Engineering students contributed to three of the four main track–winning teams.

title: 2026 Awards and Accolades
summary: An ongoing list of 2026 accolades and research awards received by Baskin Engineering faculty, students, and staff.

title: Baskin Engineering MESA student wins STEM entrepreneurship contest
summary: Gabriel Garcia Gonzalez, a UC Santa Cruz undergrad studying robotics engineering, claimed the top title the 2025 MESA Idea Accelerator alongside team members Alicia Gan of Southwestern College and Ameil Ramirez of Sacramento State. The months-long business pitch competition empowers undergraduate students to become innovative entrepreneurs. Using the Human-Centered Design process, students gain the skills...

title: Baskin Engineering MESA students connect with industry at Student Leadership Conference event
summary: A group of talented UC Santa Cruz MESA students connected with industry professionals in San Francisco during an invite-only STEM conference. MESA’s Student Leadership Conference brought together hand-picked engineering and computer science students with industry to develop the next generation of STEM leaders. Held at the San Francisco Marriott Marquis, this year’s conference challenged students...

title: We’re going to need a new innovation ecosystem
summary: The UC system, and other universities across the country are pushing hard to restore federal government funding. Ideally, our advocacy pays off such that sufficient levels of research funding continue to reach universities in support of basic research. Because, without federal support, it’s not just our researchers and students who will suffer—it’s industry, too, and the wider economy and society.

title: Generative AI Center welcomes new industry collaborations
summary: The recently launched Generative AI Center at UC Santa Cruz, based at both the main campus and at the UCSC Silicon Valley campus in Santa Clara, is bringing together university researchers and industry partners for collaboration, innovation, and impact at the cutting edge of the field.   The Center welcomes new industry partners to expand their...
